name: Glycerin (Glycerol)
description: A simple three-carbon polyol that is the most effective humectant available for DIY formulations — drawing moisture from the environment to hydrate skin while also serving as a solvent, preservative booster, and emollient.
appearance: Clear, colorless, viscous liquid; odorless with sweet taste
solubility: Fully miscible with water and ethanol; insoluble in oils
chemistry: Three hydroxyl groups hydrogen-bond with water. Modulates aquaporin-3 channels in keratinocytes. Reduces water activity (aW) in formulations. Hygroscopic — draws moisture from atmosphere above 65% RH.
warnings: In very dry environments (<40% RH), can draw moisture FROM skin — always pair with an occlusive. Sticky above 20% concentration. Use USP grade only for personal care.
shelfLife: 2–3 years sealed; indefinite if kept dry
